items.title: What happened when a rare bird found a place to nest near a logging coupe

children.content: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

gallery.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

thumbnail.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

items.title: Historic farm turns Sunday roast into paddock-to-plate dining experience

children.content: A 200-year-old Tasmanian farm is opening its gates to diners for the first time, as the family-run business seeks to expand its offerings and keep on top of costly upkeep.

image.altText: a female farmer serves two large bowls of vegetables to a table of women

items.title: Man tied up, house burnt down as thieves steal 9 guns, car

children.content: Emergency services called to a house fire at Tasmania's Coles Bay find the home owner tied up and his home torched after thieves made off with nine guns and his car.

image.altText: Rifles on display at Tasmanian firearms seller.

items.title: Government to crack down on antisemitism on university campuses

children.content: The new standards will require institutions to adopt definitions of antisemitism, racism against Aboriginal and Torres Straight Islander peoples, and Islamophobia.

image.altText: a wide image of sandstone arches at a university campus. people can be seen walking alongside the arches

items.title: Jewish staff and students speak out as unis probed at antisemitism royal commission

children.content: Jewish academics and students say they do not feel safe on campuses, as the antisemitism royal commission prepares to hold a week of hearings scrutinising universities.

image.altText: The view through an archway and onto a university courtyard

items.title: Young woman stopped taking the pill because of mental health concerns

children.content: Two young women say taking the pill to manage painful periods had side effects they weren't expecting.

items.title: Businesses caught between Labor's trust tax and stamp duty

children.content: Small businesses affected by the change are in limbo as states decide whether to waive stamp duty fees, with industry groups warning relief will be limited unless they sign on.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: Labor scoring own goals while Coalition distracted by One Nation threat

children.content: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road

children.content: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

articles.title: Why Norway feels 'bitter' after contest of controversy against England

children.content: Norway felt England's equaliser shouldn't have stood after the ball appeared to get a deflection off a wire holding up the "spidercam" above the pitch, directly leading to England gaining possession and scoring.

image.altText: Norway players surround the referee

articles.title: Swiss coach blasts 'unacceptable' VAR rule after World Cup elimination

children.content: Switzerland is fuming after Breel Embolo was sent of the highly charged World Cup quarterfinal against Argentina in Kansas City.

image.altText: Referee Joao Pinheiro shows a red card to Breel Embolo of Switzerland at the World Cup.

articles.title: Quick hits: A tearful red, a perfect 10 and a massive cast

children.content: Norway fumes at England's equaliser, Breel Embolo's tearful red card and Jordan Henderson's heavy-duty cast. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

items.title: Lindsey Graham, pivotal US Republican senator, dies suddenly at 71

children.content: US senator Lindsey Graham, a key Republican who went from a vocal critic of Donald Trump to one of his most loyal allies, has died at age 71.

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

children.content: Former NRL player Jai Arrow says he is determined to raise money and awareness for Motor Neurone Disease until a cure is found.

items.title: Family of Perth man raises questions over his Bali death

children.content: The family of Perth man Cameron Hughes, who died in a Bali immigration detention centre, says it has many questions about the circumstances surrounding his death.

items.title: Lions thump the Bombers to grab top-four spot

children.content: Brisbane Lions have taken a big win over Essendon to snatch a top-four spot after round 18, as Melbourne and the Western Bulldogs enjoy their respective victories. Follow live.

items.title: Owner taken to court over $1m damage bill to iconic 110yo cafe

children.content: The heritage-listed former Paragon Cafe in Katoomba was once considered a success story of Greek entrepreneurship, but has now landed in the NSW Land and Environment Court.

image.altText: A black and white photo of the Paragon Cafe in Katoomba.

items.title: Theoretical 'mirror life' could grow out of control, scientists fear

children.content: This year, scientists included a new potential threat in their decision to move the Doomsday Clock closer to midnight. So what is 'mirror life' and why are they concerned?

items.title: Man wanted over alleged attempted murder of 74yo Sydney man

items.title: UK police arrest 28-year-old man in Ann Widdecombe murder case

children.content: Police previously arrested, then later released, a 26-year-old man in relation to the alleged murder, after Ann Widdecombe's body was found in her residence on Thursday.

items.title: 'Fierce attack': Victim refuses to clean antisemitic graffiti off property

items.title: Junior doctor faces court over death of man in Fremantle

children.content: A junior doctor from Perth's western suburbs appears in court charged with manslaughter following the death of a 63-year-old man near Fremantle's entertainment strip in the early hours yesterday.

children.content: Australian house prices have recorded their biggest monthly fall since 2022. For young buyers hoping it's a turning point, economists have some bad news.

items.title: In this friendship group there are 'no Jews, no Muslims, just one big family'

items.title: Man charged after filming inside Dezi Freeman's hideout

items.title: Time to bite the bullet? Unpopular decision looms over $600m Aboriginal gallery

children.content: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

items.title: Teenage boy charged with murder of Victorian girl Layla Jeffery

children.content: The town of Donald in Victoria's west is in mourning, after remains believed to be missing 13-year-old Layla Jeffery were found by police on Thursday.

image.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: China ballistic missile test pushes case for Pacific security pact

children.content: Pacific Minister Pat Conroy says the testing of a nuclear-capable missile damaged China's reputation and has strengthened the case for a Pacific security pact.

image.altText: Pat Conroy at press conference

items.title: Premier Roger Cook takes over defence industries in cabinet reshuffle

children.content: WA Premier Roger Cook will take on the defence industries and tourism portfolios in a cabinet reshuffle, following the departure of longstanding minister Paul Papalia.

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook stands behind a podium, with flags hanging behind him.

items.title: Nosková claims maiden slam title at Wimbledon

children.content: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.
